observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to correct the misclassification of various items of unpaid obligations totaling 3.142 million in line with Section 113 of PD 1445 and Coa Circular No 2015-009 to come up with a more accurate and reliable financial information.

We recommended that the Management facilitate fair presentation of account presented in the financial statements by: a. instructing the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE items , including those currently in progress; and b. directing both the GSO and Accounting Office to continously reconcile the results of the physical count of PPE with the accounting records.

A. We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to reclassify the subject PPE items to their appropriate specific PPE accounts and the corresponding accumulated depreciation accoutns to ensure transparency of the accounts presented and the fair presentation of the account balances in the financial statements.
B.We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to recompute the depreciation of Road Networks in compliance with COA Circular No 2015-008 by not providing salvage value to the respective assets and effect the necessary adjusting entry to accurately restate the net book value of the assets.
C.We recommend that the Management facilitate fair presentation of account presented in the financial statements by: a. instructing the Inventory Committee to conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all PPE items , including those currently in progress; and b. directing both the GSO and Accounting Office to continously reconcile the results of the physical count of PPE with the accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare general ledgers and subsidiary ledgers to determine, at any given times, the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the financial statements.

We recommended that the management maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by accelerating the implementation of pending projects, identifying the root causes of non-implementation, and developing practical,targeted solutions to ensure the effective execution of developmental initiatives.

We recommended that the Management instruct the MDRRMO to evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate their implementation and judiciously plan on how to significantly increase the utilization of the LDRRMF fund to achieve goals/ targets of the municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery and risk reduction and management.

We recommend that the Management through the Municipal Accountant, investigate the source of the excess cash back up, and revert the same to the proper books to ensure that the of the trust fund reflects only actual unutilized balances and that the surplus is made available for other social services identified by the local Sangunian in pursuance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

observations.reason: Collectors were unable to remit some collections due to uncontrollable circumstances. The Municipal Treasurer issued Orders to the Collectors for their strict compliance regarding their liquidation of collections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assesor's Officer to submit all the lacking documents on the purchased lot pursuant to COA Circular No 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 to ensure that the disbursement are valid, legal and appropriate; and b. enjoin all concerned officials not to approve the transactions and disbursements of government funds without the availability of basic documentary requirements outlines in the aforementioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: Immediate submission of the necessary documents and moving forward, ensure strict supervision and monitoring on disbursements of government funds to ensure transactions are supported with complete documents.
observations.reason: Documents were submitted to the auditors office as requested.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, moving forward, ensure that no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is included in the APP pursuant to Section 7 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to foster meticulous and judicious procurement planning and also to facilitate monitoring and evaluation of the procurements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: To implement the recommendation in CY 2024 and years thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, expedite the preparation of the liquidation reports and immediately submit the same to the Source Agencies in compliance with COA Circular No 94-013 and to prevent possible misuse.
observations.managementAction: To implement the recommendation in CY 2024 and years thereafter.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office prepared necessary adjusting entries . Moving forward, proper recording procedures will be observed to record liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, cease from the practice of immediately issuing the Certificate of Final Acceptance prior to the lapse of the defect's liability period, or 1 year from the project completion date, to ensure possible recourse for the LGU in cases of uncorrected defects and to protect Governement's resources and property against loss or wastage, in compliance with 62.2 of the 2016 Revused IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: To implement the recommendation in CY 2024 and years thereafter.
observations.reason: On going coordination and feedbacking with concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the contractor to post warranty security after the issuance Certificate of Final Acceptance which is one year from the project completion date or the defect's liability period pursuant to Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Non- Policy Matter (NPM) No. 120-2017 dated December 29,2017 to safeguard the interests of the LGU and/or general public in the use of infrastructure projects of the government.
observations.managementAction: To implement the recommendation in CY 2024 and years thereafter.
observations.reason: On going coordination and feedbacking with concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the BAC to ensure that the documents forming the part of the contract shall be provided by the winning bidder within the required time as provided under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure safety in the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: To implement the recommendation in CY 2024 and years thereafter.
observations.reason: On going coordination and feedbacking with concerned offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
a. ensure that all identified development projects embodied in the Annual Investment Program are supported with concrete plans and designs, without any hindering issues and procurement- and- implementation-ready, and including conduct of detailied engineering for infrastructure projects, in order to complete them within the target completion date to maximize the benefit for its intended beneficiaries; b. prepare a holistic and comprehensive but realistic catch-up plan,after conducting proper monitoring and folloe-up activities, to expedite the project implementation of PPs funded especially from prior years' appropriation, and which are still at the agency pipeline, to reduce backlogs; c. review the balances of the continuing appropriations as part of the annual budget preparation process and consider the need for reprogramming of the PPAs from prior years appropriations, when necessary after determinationofamorerelevantandresponsive and procurement and implementation ready projects that may be included under the 20% DF of the Municipality in adherence with applicable laws, rules and regulations; and d. require a certificartion from the Engineering Office that the detailed engineering surveys and designs have been conducted for infrastructures projects to ensure readiness of the procurement at hand, pursuant to Item 5 Annex A of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: On going coordination and feedbacking with concerned offices; some measures implemented, others still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to: a. require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% DF,including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target pursuant to Article 454 (d) of the Implementing rules and Regulations of Local Government Code of 1991 (RA 7160) to facilitate proper monitoring of implementation of these projects; and b. seek the approval of the Sanggunian before the implementation of a specific project, in the event that specific/itemized details of the project was not identified during the budget preparation in compliance with the requirements of the related rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: a. require the Municipal Development Council to specify the details of the identified priority projects to be charged under the 20% DF,including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target pursuant to Article 454 (d) of the Implementing rules and Regulations of Local Government Code of 1991 (RA 7160) to facilitate proper monitoring of implementation of these projects; and b. seek the approval of the Sanggunian before the implementation of a specific project, in the event that specific/itemized details of the project was not identified during the budget preparation in compliance with the requirements of the related rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Projects were specified with accurate details to ensure proper monitoring of the status of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management : a. through the MDRRMO together with the concerned implementing offices, facilitate the full implementation of the unimplemented PPAs, to fully attain the objectives of the disaster risk management activities of the LDRRM Council; b. review and evaluate the need- based requirement of the PPAs by taking into consideration the timeliness, funding resources, POW and detailed engineering of infrastructure projects, as well as procurement schedule, which shall serve as performance indicator in determining whether the expected output were fully implemented or delivered as planned; and c. in the event that said PPAs are no longer viable after the conduct of close monitoringand evaluation, consider the reprogramming of PPAs and prepare and submit an ammended LDRRMFIP for approval by the Sanggunian bayan.
observations.managementAction: a. through the MDRRMO together with the concerned implementing offices, facilitate the full implementation of the unimplemented PPAs, to fully attain the objectives of the disaster risk management activities of the LDRRM Council; b. review and evaluate the need- based requirement of the PPAs by taking into consideration the timeliness, funding resources, POW and detailed engineering of infrastructure projects, as well as procurement schedule, which shall serve as performance indicator in determining whether the expected output were fully implemented or delivered as planned; and c. in the event that said PPAs are no longer viable after the conduct of close monitoringand evaluation, consider the reprogramming of PPAs and prepare and submit an ammended LDRRMFIP for approval by the Sanggunian bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and to properly and completely prepare the corresponding RPCPPE. We also recommended that the Accounting and General Services Offices maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all PPE accounts, to be reconciled with the RPCPPE to obtain the accurate value of the PPE and such be presented fairly in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The annual physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU was not yet conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee ensure that annual physical inventory is conducted for all the PPE and Inventories of the Municipality and to properly and completely prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI. We also recommended that the Management maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards for all PPE Accounts, and Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards for Inventory Accounts and such shall be reconciled with the RCPPE and RCPI to obtain the accurate values of the PPE and Inventory for its fair presentation in the financial statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the documents or records that support the adjustments made on the Due to NGAs, Due to LGU and Other Payables accounts during the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer only the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to Items 5.1.10 COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, the DRRMF – Capital Outlay amounting to P6,491,125.00 should be reverted to the General Fund, until the projects/ activities relative thereto are completed.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the significant amount of collection/receipts be deposited immediately to the authorized depository bank in compliance with Section 62 (C) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to those with outstanding cash advances and require their immediate liquidation.

We recommended that the Management exert extra effort to recover the cash shortage and intensify their collection efforts in the uncollected receivable under the livelihood program.  It is further recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the inactive recipients. Moreover, analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and may request for the write off of the dormant Receivable accounts, except for the receivable arising from cash shortage, as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Treasurer/Market Supervisor, ensure that the details in the Public Market Stall/Lot Lease Contracts (terms and conditions) are properly accomplished with complete information.  Likewise, copies of fully accomplished contracts should be submitted in compliance with COA Circular 2019-005.

We recommended that the Management provide adequate control over the acceptance of fully accomplished official receipts/sales invoices from the payee/supplier to ensure that payments are properly acknowledged.

We recommended that the Management cautiously review the terms and provisions of the performance bonds being posted by the winning bidder to ensure that these are consistent and valid for the contract.
We recommended that the Management ensure that complete documentary requirements are submitted to support the propriety of procurement process undertaken.

We recommended that the Management require the respective contractors to undertake the repair works at their own expense or deduct the corresponding amount of defects from the claim of the contractors, if any. Otherwise, the personnel in charge of the project will be held liable of the said amount.

We recommended that the LDRRMO/LDRRMC, in the preparation and review of LDRRMFIP, consider the inclusion of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, which were transferred to the Trust Fund.  This will help support the disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council within the next five (5) years.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should ensure the timely submission of accounts to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all CIP to support its balance and to facilitate the review of the accuracy and completeness of transactions related to the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and to properly and completely prepare the corresponding RPCPPE. We also recommended that the Accounting and General Services Offices maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all PPE accounts, to be reconciled with the RPCPPE to obtain the accurate value of the PPE and such be presented fairly in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all CIP to support its balance and to facilitate the review of the accuracy and completeness of transactions related to the account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the responsible officials to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements of COA Circular 2015-008 for the fair presentation of the Local Road Network account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management facilitate the preparation of documentary requirements to secure titles in the name of the municipality on the sixteen untitled lots. The Land and Land Rights recorded in the books which were not supported with TCT and other legal documents of ownership should be properly disclosed in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the verification of the existence of the “Others” account reported under the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts amounting to P17,710,202.89 and P981,819.38, respectively. Likewise, reconcile its records with the Source Agencies for the fund transfers received and thereafter, make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Accounts Payable balance with that of the supporting schedule and consequently, make the necessary adjusting entry to present fairly the balance of the AP account in the financial statement. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the Accounts Payable balance be supported with complete documentation, properly certified/ approved by concerned Heads of Offices and other sufficient documentary evidence to show proof that the subject goods/services were already delivered/rendered to qualify as valid claims against the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain and make available the General Ledgers of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts and the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount of tax due and collectible for the year that would support the balances of the related receivable accounts in the Financial  Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile cash per record and cash on hand daily and deposit all collections intact daily or as soon as the collections reach P10,000.00 to the authorized depository bank.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the account Due to LGU (Provincial Government of Cagayan) and reconcile its record with the Province on the difference noted in the details and balances. Also, prepare an adjusting entry, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the transactions recorded in the Other Professional Services account and subsequently reclassify the recording of the payment of wages of job orders into the appropriate expense accounts for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances through compliance with the regulations as provided in COA Circular 97-002 and PD 1445. We also recommended that demand letters be sent to those with outstanding cash advances and require its immediate liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that at year-end the unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE shall be transferred to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRMF” in the Trust Fund books pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 21 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 10121.

We recommended to the Management the following: (a) Require the MDRRMC to prepare the LDRRMFIP by adopting the prescribed format in the Annex A of the COA Circular 2012-002; (b) Require the MDRRMC to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP to include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; (c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and certify as correct the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in Annex B of the aforementioned Circular; and (d) Require the MDRRO to submit to the Auditor the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC.

We recommended that the Management make an extra effort to recover the cash shortage and intensify their collection efforts in the uncollected receivables under the livelihood program.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the inactive recipients. Moreover, analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and may request for the write-off of the dormant Receivable accounts, except for the receivable arising from cash shortage, as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with COA Circular No. 2010-001 on the submission of complete schedules to facilitate the conduct of necessary audit procedures that would validate management assertions in the Financial Statements of the Agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant facilitate the submission of the complete accounts of the Agency within the prescribed period in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-006. Moreover, the agency officials concerned should identify the reasons for the delayed submission of accounts and take necessary actions to address the same to ensure compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

To enhance further the existing guidelines of the Agency, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider enjoining the concerned department/s to prepare specific written criteria or policies and procedures in the grant of financial assistance to AICS Clients that will fit to the operations of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management consider formulating Policy Guidelines for the hiring of personnel under contract services/job orders. Likewise, we recommended that Management ensure that the policy guidelines formulated would not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the Municipality but promote optimum utilization of financial resources.

We recommended that the BAC consider adopting the strategy of consolidating similar items to be procured into a single undertaking, to minimize the use of alternative modes of procurement, and to avail of volume discount for bulk purchases.

We recommended to the Management the following; (a) The Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Engineer to properly plan and program the immediate implementation of the subject infrastructure projects to carry out the purpose to which these were intended (b) Ensure that the issues on needing clearance and road right of way be resolved so as not to hinder the timely execution of the projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant undertake the reconciliation of the variance between the balances per books and PPE Lapsing Schedule and make the necessary adjustments thereon to present fairly the balance of PPE account in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify the completeness of the supporting documents before approving the disbursement vouchers for repairs in order to unsure veracity of the expenses incurred.
Further, we recommended management to designate a technically and   knowledgeable employee to conduct the pre and post-inspection activities of motor vehicles/equipment subject for repair to ensure the validity the repair to be made.  Likewise, the spare parts replaced should be included in Report of Waste Materials and form part as supporting document of the disbursement voucher.

We recommended  management make representation with the court and seek possible legal remedies  to recover the  amount of shortage from the defaulting officer in order to have additional resources in implementing beneficial programs and projects of the municipality.

We recommended that management prioritize and facilitate the processing of documents in order to complete the requirements for the titling. They should coordinate with the concerned government agencies to facilitate titling of the lot, so that the ownership thereto could be properly secured and to avoid future litigations of third party against the municipality. Exert effort to retrieve the documents for its titling.

We recommended that management request the Sangguniang Bayan for the approval of a resolution effecting the transfer the ownership of the school buildings and day care centers to recipient school/barangay in order to establish proper accountability. The transfer should likewise be covered by a  Deed of Donation executed between the recipients and the Municipality of Baggao.

We recommended that management send demand letters to all loan borrowers, and/or refer to legal office for the necessary action to enforce collection thereof.  If collection is nil, management should request for write off to COA in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that management  require BAC Secretariat to post the Notice of Award within three (3) calendar days from the issuance and the corresponding Notice to Proceed and its Approved Contracts within fifteen (15) calendar days from the issuance of the Notice to Proceed in the websites of the PHILGEPS pursuant to existing regulations.

We recommended that management coordinate with the General    Service Office to give priority and strategize the conduct of  physical count all properties  found in station in order to ascertain the completeness and existence of the PPE.          

We further recommended that the General Service Office shall   see   to  it  that  all  Acknowledgement  Receipts  for Property are renewed every three years pursuant to Section 492 of the Government    Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I in order to establish property accountability.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere of Section 51 of NGAS Manual, Volume II and Section 64 of P.D. 1445 on the submission of CRAAF to the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.
